metadata.confidence_note: Evidence-only consolidation. Confidence/status signals (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW, ACTIVE/DORMANT, implemented/aspirational-unimplemented, INFERRED, VERSION UNKNOWN, soft-reference, RC-002 dead code) are preserved verbatim from source fragments. No forward engineering; no new capabilities/entities/APIs invented. Cross-links are emitted only where a source file supports them.

actors.name: Customer / Buyer
actors.description: Self-service storefront user who browses the catalog (items, types, brands), manages their own shopping basket (add items, adjust quantities), transfers anonymous basket to account on login, places orders and views order history, and manages payment methods and shipping address. Permission Level: Standard. Original variants: 'Customer', 'Buyer', 'Customer / Buyer'.

evidence.anchor: Customer / Buyer: Self-service role - browses the catalog, manages their own basket, and places their own orders

actors.name: Anonymous Shopper
actors.description: Unauthenticated storefront visitor who can browse the catalog and build an anonymous basket prior to login. The anonymous basket is later merged into the registered account on login. Original variants: 'anonymous user', 'anonymous shopper', 'anonymous session'.

evidence.anchor: Add Item to Basket trigger: 'Customer (or anonymous user) selects a catalog item to purchase'

actors.name: Administrator
actors.description: Back-office role who creates, updates, and deletes catalog items, manages catalog brands and types, and views/manages application data via the Blazor admin interface, separate from the customer-facing storefront. Permission Level: Admin.

processes.name: Browse Catalog
processes.description: Customer views available catalog items, filtering by brand or type. Value-add stage of the Customer Browse-to-Order value stream. Note: this stage is described in the value stream but is not detailed in the process models document (no step-level breakdown in evidence).
processes.trigger: Customer chooses to view available catalog items
steps: Customer views available catalog items, filtering by brand or type

evidence.anchor: Stage 1. Browse Catalog | Customer | Customer views available catalog items, filtering by brand or type | Value-Add Yes

processes.name: Add Item to Basket
processes.description: Add the requested catalog item to the customer's (or anonymous user's) basket so the basket contains the requested item with the correct quantity. Consolidates quantity if the item is already present.
processes.trigger: Customer (or anonymous user) selects a catalog item to purchase
steps: System - Retrieve the customer's existing basket, or create a new one if none exists (Decision: does a basket already exist for this user? YES -> use existing; NO -> create new)

System - Add the item to the basket, consolidating quantity if the item is already present (Decision: is the catalog item already in the basket? YES -> increase quantity; NO -> add a new basket line item)
System - Persist the updated basket

evidence.anchor: Process: Add Item to Basket - Business Rules Applied: BR005

BR005: IF a customer adds a catalog item already present in their basket THEN increase the quantity of the existing basket line instead of creating a new line; ELSE add a new basket line item (Basket.cs)

processes.name: Transfer Anonymous Basket to Registered User
processes.description: Merge an anonymous user's basket into the now-registered user's basket so the registered user's basket contains all items from their anonymous session.
processes.trigger: An anonymous user logs in or registers
steps: System - Retrieve the anonymous user's basket (Decision: does the anonymous basket exist? YES -> continue; NO -> end, nothing to transfer)
System - Retrieve or create a basket for the now-registered user (Decision: does the registered user already have a basket? YES -> merge into existing; NO -> create new)
System - Copy each item from the anonymous basket into the user's basket

evidence.anchor: Process: Transfer Anonymous Basket to Registered User - Business Rules Applied: BR005
Stage 3. Authenticate (if needed) - anonymous basket is transferred to their account

processes.name: Adjust Basket
processes.description: Customer adjusts quantities or removes items before checkout. Value-add stage of the Customer Browse-to-Order value stream. Quantity adjustments cannot produce negative values (BR007); zero-quantity lines are removed (BR006). Note: appears as a value-stream stage; not detailed as a standalone process in the process models document.
processes.trigger: Customer reviews their merged/finalized basket before checkout
steps: Customer adjusts quantities or removes items before checkout

evidence.anchor: Stage 4. Adjust Basket | Customer | Customer adjusts quantities or removes items before checkout | Value-Add Yes
BR007: negative basket quantity rejected (BasketItem.cs); BR006: zero-quantity basket line removed (Basket.cs)

processes.name: Checkout / Place Order
processes.description: Convert the customer's basket into a confirmed order with snapshotted item details, a buyer ID, a shipping address, and a calculated total. Checkout is blocked if the basket is empty.
processes.trigger: Customer initiates checkout from their basket
steps: System - Retrieve the customer's basket with items
System - Verify the basket exists and is not empty (Decision: is the basket empty? YES -> block checkout and raise empty-basket error; NO -> continue)
System - Retrieve full catalog item details for each basket item
System - Create a snapshot of each ordered item (name, picture, price) for the order record
System - Create the order with the buyer ID, shipping address, and order items
System - Calculate the order total from item prices and quantities

evidence.anchor: Process: Checkout / Place Order - Business Rules Applied: BR009, BR010, BR011, BR012

BR012: block checkout for empty basket (GuardExtensions.cs); BR010: order total = sum(unit price * quantity) (Order.cs); BR011: order requires buyer ID (Order.cs); BR009: order line requires valid catalog item ID/name/picture (CatalogItemOrdered.cs)

entities.name: CatalogItem
entities.description: A product offered for sale, with name, description, price, image, brand and type classification, plus stock-management fields (AvailableStock, RestockThreshold, MaxStockThreshold, OnReorder). Canonical source of truth for live product data.

verification_discrepancy.finding: Attributes AvailableStock, RestockThreshold, MaxStockThreshold, OnReorder are NOT present in the real source (github.com/dotnet-architecture/eShopOnWeb, main, src/ApplicationCore/Entities/CatalogItem.cs declares only Name, Description, Price, PictureUri, CatalogTypeId, CatalogBrandId). Likely contaminated from eShopOnContainers or an older variant. Treat these four fields as DISCREPANCY/do-not-generate. The derived StockReorderTriggered (EVT-12) and any reorder capability/process are consequently invalid. See EVIDENCE_VERIFICATION_REPORT.md.
